primary_function: "Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions \"deck,\" \"slides,\" \"presentation,\" or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill."

authoritative: Slide visual inspection and quality assurance
Chart and data visualization in slides

suggested_aec_gates: Verify output avoids generic blue color schemes and uses topic-specific palettes
Confirm no slides contain only text without visual elements
Validate that accent lines under titles are never used
Ensure slide titles are 36-44pt and body text is 14-16pt for proper size contrast
Verify color choices follow dominance principle with one primary color at 60-70% weight
Confirm visual QA process includes subagent inspection and conversion to images
Validate that placeholder text like 'xxxx', 'lorem', or layout references are removed
Ensure minimum 0.5 inch margins and consistent 0.3-0.5 inch spacing between elements
Verify typography avoids defaulting to Arial and uses intentional font pairings
Confirm each slide includes at least one visual element beyond text
trigger_text: "Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions \"deck,\" \"slides,\" \"presentation,\" or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill."
